description: In a passive clause, 'se' promotes the patient of an action to subject position and allows the agent to go unexpressed. Attention stays with the affected participant or the result.
articulatory_notes: A central groove in the tongue directs air through 's', then the tongue relaxes into true-mid front 'e'. The complete form is a fully stressed monosyllable.
usage_notes: Voice follows aspect and precedes evidentiality. Within that rank, 'se ka' is the only permitted pair and its order is fixed. Apart from this pairing, a clause uses a single voice particle.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The passive can centre the person or thing affected before a conversation turns to who acted. Because it may leave the agent unsaid, responsibility sometimes needs its own next sentence.
